response.justification: The role requires 5–10 years of hands-on C++ development on Windows, strong SQL Server/Oracle experience, and fixed-income or capital-markets domain knowledge. The candidate's C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments plus a university thesis project, far short of the requirement, and the bulk of their career is web/backend work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience (only MySQL/MongoDB/generic SQL) and no fixed-income or investment-banking C++ work; the RIDE Capital fintech role was Node/GraphQL, not C++. The mismatch on every mandatory technical criterion makes this candidate a poor fit.
strengths: Broad, long software engineering career across web, mobile, and embedded
Some financial-services exposure via RIDE Capital investment platform
concerns: Only ~6 months of C++ experience versus the required 5–10 years
No SQL Server/Oracle or fixed-income/capital-markets C++ domain work
Core stack is web/JS/DLang, not Windows C++ systems development
